logical-reasoning.q: Dog is to puppy as cat is to ___?
logical-reasoning.e: A baby dog is a puppy, and a baby cat is a kitten.

logical-reasoning.q: Which one is different: Apple, Banana, Carrot, Orange?
logical-reasoning.e: Carrot is a vegetable. Apple, Banana, and Orange are fruits.

logical-reasoning.q: If A=1, B=2, C=3, what is the value of CAT?
Which letter comes exactly in the middle of the alphabet?
logical-reasoning.e: M is the 13th letter. There are 26 letters, so M is in the middle.

logical-reasoning.q: In a queue of 5 children, Riya is 2nd from the front. What is her position from the back?
logical-reasoning.e: In a queue of 5, 2nd from front means 4th from back.

logical-reasoning.q: Pen is to write as knife is to ___?
logical-reasoning.e: We use a pen to write and a knife to cut.

logical-reasoning.q: Which one is different: Square, Circle, Triangle, Rectangle?
logical-reasoning.e: Circle has no corners. Square, Triangle, and Rectangle have corners.

logical-reasoning.q: How many days are in a week?
logical-reasoning.e: There are 7 days in a week: Sunday to Saturday.

logical-reasoning.q: How many months are in a year?
logical-reasoning.e: There are 12 months in a year.

logical-reasoning.q: You have a red shirt and a blue shirt. You have black pants and white pants. How many different outfits can you make?
logical-reasoning.e: Red+Black, Red+White, Blue+Black, Blue+White = 4 outfits.

logical-reasoning.q: What comes next: 2, 4, 6, 8, ___?
logical-reasoning.e: The pattern adds 2 each time. 8 + 2 = 10.

logical-reasoning.q: Which one is different: Dog, Cat, Cow, Lion?
logical-reasoning.e: Lion is a wild animal. Dog, Cat, and Cow are domestic animals.

logical-reasoning.q: If A=1, B=2, C=3, what is the value of BAD?
Which letter comes after G in the alphabet?
logical-reasoning.e: The alphabet order is ... F, G, H, I, J ...

logical-reasoning.q: In a line of 7 children, Sita is 3rd from the front. What is her position from the back?
logical-reasoning.e: In a line of 7, 3rd from front = 5th from back.

logical-reasoning.q: Bird is to fly as fish is to ___?
logical-reasoning.e: Birds fly and fish swim.

logical-reasoning.q: What comes next: 5, 10, 15, 20, ___?
logical-reasoning.e: The pattern adds 5 each time. 20 + 5 = 25.

logical-reasoning.q: How many days are in February in a leap year?
logical-reasoning.e: February has 29 days in a leap year.

logical-reasoning.q: A triangle has how many sides?
logical-reasoning.e: A triangle has 3 sides.

logical-reasoning.q: You can choose juice or milk. You can choose bread or rice. How many different meals can you make?
logical-reasoning.e: Juice+Bread, Juice+Rice, Milk+Bread, Milk+Rice = 4 meals.

logical-reasoning.q: Book is to read as song is to ___?
logical-reasoning.e: We read books and sing songs.

logical-reasoning.q: What comes next: 1, 3, 5, 7, ___?
logical-reasoning.e: The pattern adds 2 each time. 7 + 2 = 9.

logical-reasoning.q: A square has how many corners (vertices)?
logical-reasoning.e: A square has 4 corners.

logical-reasoning.q: Which letter comes before D in the alphabet?
logical-reasoning.e: The alphabet order is ... A, B, C, D, E ...

logical-reasoning.q: Which one is different: Red, Blue, Green, Apple?
logical-reasoning.e: Apple is a fruit. Red, Blue, and Green are colours.

plants.q: Which part of a plant grows underground?
What do plants need to make food?
What do bees collect from flowers?
Which plant do we get rice from?
What do plants need from the soil?
What part of a plant is usually green?
Which part of a plant carries water to the leaves?
plants.e: The stem carries water to the leaves.

plants.q: What do plants give out that we breathe?
Which part of a plant becomes a fruit?
What gas do plants take in for photosynthesis?
Which of these is a vegetable?
Which of these is a fruit?
Where do plants get their green colour from?
plants.e: Chlorophyll gives plants their green colour.

plants.q: What do we call the process by which plants make food?
Which part of a plant absorbs water from soil?
Which plant do we get wheat from?
Do plants need sunlight to grow?
Which part of a plant holds the seeds?
What do we call a young plant?
plants.e: A young plant is called a seedling.

plants.q: Which of these grows from a seed?
What do plants need besides sunlight and water?
plants.e: Plants need air and nutrients from soil.

plants.q: Which plant part is used to make paper?
plants.e: Paper is made from plant stems and leaves.

plants.q: What do we call plants that live for one year?
plants.e: Plants that live one year are annuals.

plants.q: Which of these is a flowering plant?
What do plants release at night?
plants.e: Plants release carbon dioxide at night.

plants-and-animals.q: Which part of a plant grows underground?
What do plants need to make food?
Which animal gives us milk?
What do we call the home of a bird?
Which animal has a long trunk?
Which animal barks?
What do we call a baby horse?
plants-and-animals.e: A baby horse is a foal.

plants-and-animals.q: Which animal can fly?
What do bees collect from flowers?
Which plant do we get rice from?
What do we call a baby dog?
plants-and-animals.e: A baby dog is a puppy.

plants-and-animals.q: Which animal has a shell?
What do caterpillars turn into?
Which animal lives in water?
What do plants need from the soil?
Which bird cannot fly?
What do we call a group of fish?
plants-and-animals.e: A group of fish is a school.

plants-and-animals.q: Which animal has stripes?
What part of a plant is usually green?
Which animal hibernates in winter?
What do frogs eat?
Which animal has a hump?
What do we get from hens?
Which insect makes honey?
What do we call a baby cat?
plants-and-animals.e: A baby cat is a kitten.

animals.q: What do we call the home of a bird?
Which animal has a long trunk?
What do we call a group of fish?
animals.e: A group of fish is a school.

animals.q: What do we call the home of a bird?
Which animal has a long trunk?
What do we call a group of fish?
animals.e: A group of fish is a school.

animals.q: Which animal has a long neck?
Which animal has black and white stripes?
animals.e: Zebras have black and white stripes.

animals.q: Which animal is known as man's best friend?
animals.e: Dogs are called man's best friend.

animals.q: Which animal is the king of the jungle?
animals.e: The lion is called the king of the jungle.

animals.q: Which animal has a curly tail?

living-and-non-living.q: Which of these is a living thing?
living-and-non-living.e: Trees are living - they grow and need food.
Rocks are non-living - they do not grow.
Living things need food to grow and stay alive.
Flowers are living and can grow.
Stones are non-living and do not need water.
Living things grow and change.
Cats are living animals.
A pencil is a non-living object.
Plants take in air through their leaves.
Humans need air to breathe.
Non-living things cannot reproduce.
Dogs are living and can move on their own.
Living things need food, water, and air.
Seeds are living - they can grow into plants.

living-and-non-living.q: Which is made by nature and is living?
living-and-non-living.e: Trees are natural and living.
Non-living things do not grow.
Plants can respond to touch (e.g. touch-me-not).
Living things grow and can reproduce.

living-and-non-living.q: Which of these is NOT living?
living-and-non-living.e: A key is a non-living object.

living-and-non-living.q: Can we make living things in a factory?
living-and-non-living.e: Living things come from other living things.
Plants need sunlight to make food.
Animals need food for energy.
Plants can reproduce (seeds, etc.).
Water is non-living - it does not grow.
Living things like humans excrete waste.

human-body.q: How many sense organs do we have?
human-body.e: We have five sense organs: eyes, ears, nose, tongue, skin.
We breathe through nose and mouth.

human-body.q: How many legs does a person have?
We have ___ bones in our body.
Oxygen is taken in by the ___.
How many fingers do we have?
human-body.e: We have 10 fingers (5 on each hand).
We need food, water, and air.

human-body.q: How many chambers does the heart have?
Which vitamin helps us see in dim light?
Which part connects mouth to stomach?

food.q: Which is a source of protein?
Which vitamin do we get from sunlight?
food.e: Vitamin D is made when skin gets sunlight.

food.q: Which food group gives quick energy?
We should drink ___ glasses of water daily.
Breakfast is the ___ meal of the day.
food.e: Breakfast gives energy for the day.
A balanced diet keeps us healthy.
Green vegetables have vitamins and minerals.

food.q: Raw vegetables should be ___ before eating.
Which is a source of omega-3?
food.e: Fish is rich in omega-3 fatty acids.

food.q: Which meal should not be skipped?

food.e: Water helps in many body functions.

air-and-water.q: Which gas do we breathe out?
Which is a source of water?
air-and-water.e: Rain is a source of water.

air-and-water.q: How many states can water exist in?
air-and-water.e: Water can be solid, liquid, or gas.
Plants need CO2 and water for photosynthesis.

air-and-water.q: Which is NOT a use of water?

air-and-water.e: Air has weight and takes up space.

air-and-water.q: Which gas do plants take in?
air-and-water.e: Plants take in CO2 for photosynthesis.

air-and-water.q: Evaporation is when water turns into ___.

air-and-water.e: We need both air and water.

air-and-water.q: Which is a form of precipitation?
air-and-water.e: Rain is a form of precipitation.
Water at room temperature is liquid.

air-and-water.q: Condensation is when vapour turns into ___.

weather-and-sky.q: How many planets are in our solar system?
weather-and-sky.e: There are 8 planets in our solar system.
The Sun rises in the east.

weather-and-sky.q: The Moon gets light from the ___.
How many days does the Moon take to orbit Earth?
Which is the closest star to Earth?
weather-and-sky.e: The Sun is the closest star.
Thunder is the sound of lightning.
We usually see the Moon at night.

weather-and-sky.q: Which planet do we live on?

weather-and-sky.e: The Sun sets in the west.

weather-and-sky.q: Which season has the most rain?

weather-and-sky.e: Stars are balls of hot gas.

weather-and-sky.q: Day and night are caused by ___.
weather-and-sky.e: Earth spinning causes day and night.
Monday is a day, not a season.
Clouds are made of water droplets.

weather-and-sky.q: How long does Earth take to orbit the Sun?
weather-and-sky.e: Earth takes 1 year to orbit the Sun.
The Moon is Earth natural satellite.

matter-and-materials.q: Which state of matter has a fixed shape?
Water in ice form is a ___.

matter-and-materials.e: Water is a liquid and can flow.
Air is a mixture of gases.

matter-and-materials.q: Melting is when solid turns to ___.
Boiling is when liquid turns to ___.
Which is a conductor of heat?
Which state has no fixed shape?
matter-and-materials.e: Liquids and gases have no fixed shape.

matter-and-materials.q: Freezing is when liquid turns to ___.

force-and-energy.q: A push or pull is called ___.
force-and-energy.e: Force is a push or pull.
The Sun gives light and heat.

force-and-energy.q: Which is a form of energy?
force-and-energy.e: Light is a form of energy.

force-and-energy.q: Which force keeps us on Earth?

force-and-energy.e: Sound needs a medium like air.

force-and-energy.q: Which pole do two magnets repel?
Which is NOT a simple machine?

force-and-energy.e: Heat flows from hot to cold.

force-and-energy.q: A stretched rubber band has ___ energy.

force-and-energy.e: Current produces heat and magnetic effect.

force-and-energy.q: Work is done when force causes ___.

earth-and-universe.q: Which layer protects us from Sun rays?
Which is NOT a natural resource?

earth-and-universe.e: Earth rotation causes day and night.
Minerals are in rocks and soil.
Coal and petroleum are fossil fuels.

our-environment.q: We should ___ to save the environment.
We should not ___ in water bodies.
